Ntc Of Tentative Ruling And Continued Hearing On Deft'S Demurrer And Mtn To Strike Portions Of Plntf'S Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-13-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Brett Watson, Esq. of Palmer, Lombardi & Donohue, 213-688-0403 for defendant and moving party American Express Travel
Kamelesh Banga, 707-552-5717, plaintiff and opposing party apearing propria persona

RULING:
ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED THE FOLLOWING RULING:
DEFENDANT AMERICAN EXPRESS TRAVEL RELATED SERVICES COMPANY'S, Demurrer And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. EACH OF THE CAUSES OF ACTION IS BASED ON THE PRODUCTION OF RECORDS IN RESPONSE TO A SUBPOENA. EVEN IF THE RECORDS WERE PRODUCED IN ERROR, THE DISCLOSURE IS PRIVILEGED UNDER CIVIL CODE SECTION 47(b).
THE MOTION TO STRIKE LIKEWISE IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION. NO PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARE RECOVERABLE UNDER THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST TO DISMISS THE CASE WITH PREJUDICE IS GRANTED. CASE DISMISSED WITH PREJUDICE. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189(302/PJM/SN-X)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-23-2007 Continued To Jan-11-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-15-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Brett D. Watson of Palmer Lombardi & Donohue, 213-688-0430, appearing for defendant and moving party

No other appearances on this matter. Mr. Watson argued as to the issue of leave to amend. As the opposing party was not present to argue leave to amend, the Court modified its ruling as follows: DEFENDANT AMERICAN EXPRESS TRAVEL RELATED SERVICES COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained as to the 1st, 2nd, 3rd, and 4th causes of action with leave to amend, subject to Mr. Watson re-noticing the motion for argument as to whether the motion may be sustained without leave to amend. (302/JJC/xSN) Judge John J. Conway, Reporter Janet Pond CSR #5292

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-15-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Brett D. Watson of Palmer Lombardi & Donohue, 213-688-0430, appearing for defendant and moving party

No other appearances on this matter. Mr. Watson argued as to the issue of leave to amend. As the opposing party was not present to argue leave to amend, the Court modified its ruling as follows: DEFENDANT AMERICAN EXPRESS TRAVEL RELATED SERVICES COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is granted with leave to amend as to causes of action 1, 2, and 4 subject to Mr. Watson re-noticing the motion for argument as to whether the motion may be granted without leave to amend. Granted without leave to amend as to cause of action 3. (302/JJC/xSN) Judge John J. Conway, Reporter Janet Pond CSR #5292
